articles.title: Jorge Gutierrez faces backlash over use of AI in 'Punky Duck'
articles.description: It took over a decade for Jorge R. Gutierrez to get approval for his 2014 film “The Book of Life.” His new project, "Punky Duck," will take less time — thanks to AI.
articles.content:
Animator Jorge R. Gutierrez is facing online backlash following news that his latest series, “Punky Duck,” will use artificial intelligence for its production.

articles.title: Filmmaker Paul Schrader lets loose in zesty keynote at AI conference
articles.description: At the rapidly expanding AI on the Lot conference in Culver City, the “Taxi Driver” scripter mixed provocation and unease as he contemplated Hollywood’s future.
articles.content:
When filmmaker Paul Schrader announced on Facebook that he would be delivering the keynote address at this year’s fourth annual AI on the Lot conference, the reaction from some fellow writers and artists was swift and hostile.

articles.title: Student commencement boos are a sign of wider AI woes
articles.description: As companies race to weave AI into nearly every industry, some college students are responding with open hostility
articles.content:
As companies race to weave AI into nearly every industry, some college students are responding with open hostility.

articles.title: AI facial recognition to check age of asylum seekers from next year
articles.description: The Home Office says the tool will make it easier to identify adult migrants "attempting to game the system".
articles.content:
An AI facial recognition tool that aims to detect adult migrants posing as children will be deployed at the UK's borders next year.
A software company has been awarded a contract to develop and test the technology, which will estimate a person's age ... [2847 chars]

articles.title: Shopping with AI leads to more sales than by traditional online searches - Adobe
articles.description: Figures for May show the UK AI shopping conversion rate was up 182% year-on-year and 543% since January last year, Adobe said.
articles.content:
Shopping with the help of AI is now leading to more sales in the UK than those via more traditional online searches, figures suggest.

articles.title: AI and drones can help improve early warning systems for Vibrio bacteria in the Baltic Sea
articles.description: The presence of the marine bacterium Vibrio vulnificus, which is potentially dangerous to humans, can now be predicted up to five weeks in advance in the Baltic Sea using artificial intelligence (AI). A research team led by the Leibniz Institute for Baltic Sea Research Warnemünde (IOW) has reached this conclusion by combining high-resolution environmental, satellite, and microbiome data in an AI-based analysis.

articles.title: AI curriculum taskforce suggest learning from industry, practical exposure for Er students
articles.description: NEW DELHI, May 28: An industry-led AI curriculum taskforce has recommended shifting the present lecture-based teaching system for technical education to learning from real industry use cases and enhancing practical exposure of students to equip them with better understanding of AI development, an official statement said on Thursday. The task force has recommended a shift from the current 25–30 per cent practical exposure to 40–75 per cent, depending on the nature of the degree and the chosen specialisation by engineering […]
